chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 794 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Application in disposed of case 1. For orders on MA No.6238/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on MA No.5324/2025 (Ex./A) 3. For orders on MA No.5325/2025 (R/Surety) 29.10.2025 Mr. Sartaj Haider, Advocate, holding brief for Mr. Abdul Jabbar Siyal, Advocate for applicant / surety..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 3. This application has been filed by the applicant seeking return of surety documents. As per contentions of learned Counsel, supported by report of Accountant of this Court, it appears that accused Karam Khan Bozdar, who on 02.09.2025, was granted protective bail for a period of seven days, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/-, for which applicant stood surety by depositing three original special savings certificates of such amount, had surrendered before the trial Court and has obtained interim pre-arrest bail, as is evident from letter dated 08.09.2025, issued by the office of the learned trial Court, annexed with this application. On the last date of hearing, learned Counsel for the applicant required time to file order obtained from the Court of learned Additional Sessions Judge-I, Ghotki. He, however, has submitted a statement along with certified copy of diary dated 08.09.2025 confirming the above facts. Mr. Khalil Ahmed Maitlo, learned Deputy Prosecutor General, present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice and, in view of the above position, extends his no objection for granting this application.

Mr. Khalil Ahmed Maitlo, learned Deputy Prosecutor General, present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice and, in view of the above position, extends his no objection for granting this application. Accordingly, the application is allowed. Office is directed to return the surety documents to the applicant after proper identification and verification, as per rules. J U D G E Abdul Basit
